React Beginner to Advanced Series — Part 7B
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Non-member ? click here to read.
In Part 7A, we discovered how React listens to user interactions through events such as onClick, onDoubleClick, onMouseEnter, and onKeyDown.
Every time a user clicked a button, typed into an input, or hovered over an element, React executed our event handler and responded instantly.
But in real-world applications, clicking a button is only the beginning.
Think about the applications you use every day.
When you log in to Gmail…

• Your email is validated.

• Empty fields show an error.

• The page doesn’t refresh.

• Your credentials are sent to the server.
When you search on Amazon…

• Results change as you type.

• Suggestions appear instantly.

• Filters update automatically.
When you create a LinkedIn profile…

• Every keystroke updates the form.

• Required fields are validated.

• The Save button becomes enabled only when the form is complete.
